title: Number of Visible People in a Queue
description:
Number of Visible People in a Queue

There are n people standing in a queue, numbered from 0 to n - 1 in left-to-right order. You are given an array heights of size n that represents the heights of the people.

Person i can see person j (where i < j) if and only if:

min(heights[i], heights[j]) > max(heights[i+1], heights[i+2], ..., heights[j-1])

In other words, both people must be taller than everyone standing between them.

Return an array answer of size n where answer[i] is the number of people person i can see to their right.

Examples:

  • Input: heights = [10, 6, 8, 5, 11, 9]
    Output: [3, 1, 2, 1, 1, 0]
    Explanation: Person 0 (h=10) can see persons 1 (h=6), 2 (h=8), and 4 (h=11). Person 2 (h=8) can see persons 3 (h=5) and 4 (h=11).
  • Input: heights = [5, 1, 2, 3, 10]
    Output: [4, 1, 1, 1, 0]

Constraints:

  • 1 <= heights.length <= 105
  • 1 <= heights[i] <= 105
  • All the values of heights are unique
solution_text:
Number of Visible People in a Queue

Algorithm (Monotonic Decreasing Stack, Right to Left):

  1. Process each person from right to left, maintaining a monotonic decreasing stack of heights seen so far
  2. For each person at index i, count how many people they can see:
    • While the stack top is shorter than heights[i], pop it — this shorter person is visible and then becomes blocked by the next taller person
    • If the stack still has an element after the while loop, that person is also visible (they are the first one taller than person i)
  3. Push heights[i] onto the stack

Time Complexity: O(n) — each height is pushed and popped at most once

Space Complexity: O(n) for the stack

Key Insights:

  • A shorter person between i and j never blocks visibility — only someone taller than both blocks the view
  • Once a height is popped (consumed by a taller person scanning left), it can never be visible to anyone further left — making each pop permanent
  • The +1 after the while loop accounts for the first blocking taller person, who is visible precisely because they terminated the scan
solution_code:
def canSeePersonsCount(heights):
    n = len(heights)
    result = [0] * n
    stack = []
    for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
        count = 0
        while stack and heights[i] > stack[-1]:
            stack.pop()
            count += 1
        if stack:
            count += 1
        result[i] = count
        stack.append(heights[i])
    return result
